title: Bookkeeper/Staff Accountant
description:
Overseas Staff Accountant

Company: Real Estate Investment Company

Location: Remote / Overseas

Employment Type: Full-Time – U.S. hours

Reports To: Controller 



Position Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and dependable Overseas Bookkeeper/Staff Accountant to support the accounting operations of a growing real estate investment company. This role is responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, processing day-to-day accounting transactions, and assisting with monthly close activities. The ideal candidate has strong experience with QuickBooks Online, excellent organizational skills, and the ability to work independently in a remote environment. Experience with real estate accounting is a plus.



Key Responsibilities



Daily / Weekly Duties

• Maintain and update company books in QuickBooks Online

• Categorize and record bank and credit card transactions accurately

• Review transactions for proper coding by property, class, and account

• Enter vendor bills, invoices, and expenses into accounting system

• Prepare and send intercompany invoices as needed

• Follow up on outstanding invoices or missing receipts/documentation

• Organize and maintain digital financial records and files



Monthly Duties

• Perform monthly bank and credit card reconciliations

• Reconcile loan balances, escrow accounts, and intercompany accounts

• Assist with month-end close and financial reporting packages

• Review P&L, balance sheet, and general ledger for discrepancies

• Prepare supporting schedules for management review



Real Estate Specific Duties

• Track income and expenses by property/entity

• Record rent income, maintenance expenses, utilities, insurance, and repairs

• Assist with owner draws, capital contributions, and distributions

• Support property acquisition/disposition accounting entries

• Maintain subcontractor workers’ compensation certificates and expirations

title: Property Management Operations & Accounting Coordinator for Residential Properties in Washington, DC
description:
We are a U.S.-based real estate investment and property management company with a portfolio of 100+ residential rental units. We are looking for a highly reliable, detail-oriented, numbers-comfortable Operations & Accounting Coordinator to support day-to-day property management, tenant communications, rent tracking, maintenance coordination, leasing support, and basic accounting workflows.



This is a long-term role for someone who is organized, calm under pressure, proactive, and consistent. We are not looking for a short-term freelancer. We are looking for someone who wants to become a trusted part of a growing real estate operations team.



About the Role



This role is ideal for someone who enjoys structure, accuracy, follow-through, and problem solving. You will help keep our operations organized by making sure tenant issues are tracked, rent/payment information is updated, maintenance requests are followed up on, and important details do not fall through the cracks.



You do not need to know everything on day one. However, you must be very detail-oriented, responsive, reliable, and willing to learn our systems and processes.



Key Responsibilities

- Operations Support

- Monitor and organize tenant, vendor, and applicant communications

- Assist with daily inbox, text, and task review

- Create, update, and track tasks in our project management system

- Follow up on open items until completed

- Flag urgent issues promptly

- Maintain clean notes and records for each property/unit

- Help ensure nothing is missed between tea
mbers

- Rent Tracking and Delinquency Support

- Review rent payment records and tenant ledgers

- Track unpaid balances, partial payments, and payment plan status

- Identify discrepancies between payment records and tenant accounts

- Prepare simple delinquency summaries

- Flag accounts needing follow-up

- Help maintain accurate internal tracking reports

- Basic Accounting and Reconciliation Support

- Assist with organizing income and expense information

- Compare ledgers, bank records, invoices, and payment logs

- Identify missing, duplicate, or inconsistent entries

- Help organize supporting documents for payments, charges, and reimbursements

- Prepare clean summaries for management review

- Support accounting VAs as needed

- Maintenance Coordination

- Track incoming maintenance requests

- Coordinate updates between tenants, technicians, vendors, and management

- Follow up on unresolved work orders

- Confirm completion status

- Request photos, invoices, or notes when needed

- Keep maintenance records updated

- Leasing Support

- Help respond to prospective tenant inquiries

- Track leads, showings, applications, and pending documents

- Assist with pre-screening questions

- Follow up with applicants in a timely and professional manner

- Maintain leasing status updates by unit

- Process and Quality Control

- Review work for accuracy before marking it complete

- Catch inconsistencies or missing information

- Suggest improvements to workflows

- Maintain checklists and SOPs

- Help create a more organized, reliable operating system



Required Skills and Qualities



The right person for this role must be:



- Highly detail-oriented

- Very organized

- Comfortable working with numbers

- Reliable and consistent with attendance and communication

- Able to follow written instructions carefully

- Able to ask questions when something is unclear

- Proactive about flagging problems

- Calm, professional, and respectful in communication

- Comfortable working independently

- Willing to learn property management systems and processes

- Able to manage multiple small tasks without losing track of details



Experience Preferred



Experience in any of the following is strongly preferred but not required:



- Property management

- Real estate administration

- Bookkeeping

- Accounting support

- Rent rolls or tenant ledgers

- Accounts receivable

- AppFolio 

- ClickUp and Asana

- Google Workspace

- Excel or Google Sheets

-
 and text-based customer service



Tools We Use



Experience with these tools is helpful, but we can train the right person:



- AppFolio

- ClickUp

- Quo (FKA OpenPhone)

- Gmail / Google Workspace

- Google Sheets

- Notion

- Slack or Google Chat

- Make



Schedule and Availability



- Full-time position preferred

- Must be available during agreed working hours

- Must provide daily updates

- Must communicate promptly if unavailable

- Long-term availability is required

- Full overlap with U.S. Eastern Time is required



We’re looking for someone who can be consistently available and engaged each day, so this position is best suited for candidates with the capacity to focus on a long-term role.



Communication Expectations



Because this role supports active property operations, communication is critical.



You must be able to:



- Confirm receipt of important messages

- Provide daily status updates

- Ask questions instead of guessing

- Notify the team early if there is a delay

- Keep task notes updated

- Communicate clearly and professionally with tenants, applicants, vendors, and tea
mbers



Reliability is one of the most important parts of this role.



What Success Looks Like



You will be successful in this role if:



- Tasks are updated and organized

- Payment issues are caught early

- Tenant and vendor follow-ups happen on time

- Maintenance requests do not sit unresolved

- Delinquency reports are accurate

- Management has clear visibility into open issues

- You communicate consistently

- You help reduce chaos, not create more of it



Compensation



Compensation will be based on experience, skill level, and performance during the paid skills test and trial period.



Shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a paid skills test before final selection.



Application Instructions



To apply, please respond with the following:



- Your relevant experience with property management, accounting, bookkeeping, operations, or administrative support

- The tools/software you have used

- Your current work schedule and number of current clients

- Your desired hourly or monthly rate

- Your internet reliability and backup plan

- A brief example of a time you caught an error or solved an operational problem

- Confirmation that you are available for long-term work and can provide daily updates



Please also include the phrase “Details Matter” at the top of your application so we know you read the full job description.



Important Note



We value kindness, professionalism, and teamwork. At the same time, this role requires consistency, accuracy, and accountability. If you are reliable, detail-oriented, good with numbers, and looking for a stable long-term role, we would love to hear from you.

title: PROPERTY MANAGEMENT COORDINATOR — US REAL ESTATE PORTFOLIO
description:
About us

Green Elm Invest is a founder-led US real estate company. We own long-term residential rentals in Pennsylvania and short-term rentals in Tennessee's Smoky Mountains. Small team, fast-moving, AI-first. We give talented people autonomy and room to lead.

The role

You'll run day-to-day operations across our entire residential portfolio. Tenant communications, guest communications, vendor coordination, maintenance triage, conflict resolution, and the SOPs that make the whole machine repeatable. You'll be the calm voice when something goes wrong — and the assertive one when a vendor is dragging.

This is not a wait-to-be-told role. You make decisions, close loops, and protect the owner's time.

What you'll own



Tenant communications (PA long-term rentals): reply within 4 hours, defuse escalations, escalate only what truly needs the owner.

Guest communications (TN short-term rentals): pre-arrival, in-stay, post-stay. 5-star service voice.

Vendor management: quotes, scheduling, access, follow-through. Hold vendors accountable.

Conflict resolution: late rent, noise complaints, vendor disputes, refund demands.

Maintenance triage and ClickUp logging.

SOPs and playbooks: when something happens twice, you write the SOP.

RentRedi, OwnerRez, and ClickUp ownership.

AI-leveraged execution — ChatGPT and Claude daily.

Daily 5-line handoff: closed, open, decisions needed.



You're a great fit if you



Are confident on the phone in clear, near-native English.

Move fast, own outcomes, and close loops without being asked.

Can cover 5:30 PM – 12 AM US Eastern (5:30 AM – 12 PM Manila).

Are AI-fluent — you actually use ChatGPT or Claude daily, can write clean prompts, build custom Projects, and judge when AI output is good vs. when to throw it out.

Are calm under pressure and protect the owner's time.



Bonus: RentRedi / OwnerRez / ClickUp experience, real estate or hospitality background, Zapier or Make automations.



What's in it for you

PTO after 90 days. Software stipend (ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro, etc.).

Direct line to the owner. Stable, profitable, long-term role.



How to apply

Send: (1) your resume, (2) a 2-minute video — who you are, where you're based, why this role, and one specific example of how you made operations more efficient (the challenge, what you did, the outcome), (3) bonus: one way you use AI today — a screenshot, a custom Claude Project link, or a Loom.

We're hiring fast. Clear, thoughtful applications stand out.

title: REAL ESTATE VIRTUAL ASSISTANT-  TENANT & VENDOR OPERATIONS
description:
Who we are

Green Elm Invest is a small US real estate company that owns long-term rentals in Pennsylvania and short-term rentals in Tennessee. We're founder-led, profitable, and unusually AI-forward for our industry. We hire people who can think and operate without supervision.

What you'll do

You'll be the operator who keeps our portfolio running. Every tenant message, every guest inquiry, every vendor coordination, every maintenance ticket — you own it from the 
nt it lands until it's closed. You'll write the SOPs that turn one-off solutions into repeatable processes, and you'll use AI tools daily to do twice the work in half the time.

We need someone assertive — not someone who waits for instructions. If you're the kind of person who closes loops without being chased, this is your seat.

Day-to-day responsibilities



Reply to every PA tenant message within 4 hours. Set the tone, calm escalations, escalate only the real ones.

Manage TN short-term rental guests: pre-arrival, in-stay, post-stay. 5-star service voice.

Coordinate vendors — plumbers, HVAC, cleaners, handymen. Get quotes, schedule, follow up, replace the ones who don't perform.

Resolve conflicts: late rent, noise issues, vendor disputes, refund requests.

Triage maintenance tickets and log everything in ClickUp.

Write SOPs for recurring situations. Maintain the operating manual.

Keep RentRedi, OwnerRez, and ClickUp clean and current.

Use ChatGPT and Claude daily to draft, summarize, and automate.

Send a 5-line end-of-day handoff: closed, open, owner-decisions needed.



Who we want



Confident phone presence in clear, near-native English.

Self-starter who closes loops without prompting.

Available 5:30 PM – 12 AM US Eastern (5:30 AM – 12 PM Manila).

AI-fluent — you actually use ChatGPT or Claude every day in your current work and can show us how.

Calm in chaos. An angry tenant or a no-show plumber doesn't rattle you.



Bonus: RentRedi / OwnerRez / ClickUp hands-on, real estate or hospitality background, Zapier / Make / n8n automation experience.

Compensation & perks

PTO after 90 days. We pay for ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro, and other tools you need.

Long-term role. Direct access to the owner. No middle management.



How to apply

Send us: (1) your resume, (2) a 2-minute video introducing yourself and walking us through one time you made operations more efficient — what was the problem, what you did, what changed, (3) bonus: share an example of how you use AI in your current work.

Strong, specific applications get reviewed first.

title: PROPERTY OPERATIONS SPECIALIST -LONG-TERM & SHORT-TERM RENTALS
description:
About Green Elm Invest

We're a founder-led US real estate company. Long-term rentals in Pennsylvania, short-term rentals in Tennessee's Smoky Mountains. Small team, smart systems, AI built into how we work. We're not a traditional landlord — we're building the back office of a modern property management company.

The opportunity

As our Property Operations Specialist, you'll be the person the portfolio runs through. You'll handle all tenant and guest communications, manage every vendor relationship, resolve every conflict, and write the playbooks that make our operations scalable. You'll use AI tools every day — and you'll bring us workflows we haven't thought of.

This role demands assertiveness, not deference. You close loops. You chase what needs to be chased. You don't ping the owner with questions you can solve in 10 minutes.

Your responsibilities



Tenant communications (PA long-term rentals): 4-hour reply window, calm tone, surgical escalations.

Guest communications (TN short-term rentals): pre-arrival to post-stay. Recover bad reviews before they post.

Vendor relationships: quote sourcing, scheduling, access coordination, performance management.

Conflict resolution: late rent, noise, vendor disputes, refund demands. Own outcome and relationship simultaneously.

Maintenance triage and ClickUp tracking.

SOPs and operating playbooks — the operating manual lives with you.

RentRedi, OwnerRez, and ClickUp daily ownership.

AI-leveraged execution — ChatGPT and Claude in your daily flow.

Daily 5-line handoff at end of shift.



What we're looking for



Strong written and spoken English. Native or near-native level.

Comfortable on the phone with vendors, tenants, and US utility companies.

US Eastern coverage: 5:30 PM – 12 AM ET (5:30 AM – 12 PM Manila).

AI-fluent — daily ChatGPT or Claude use, prompt-writing skills, custom Projects, judgment about when AI helps vs. hurts.

Owner mindset. You think about cash flow, efficiency, and long-term value.

Calm under pressure. Conflict doesn't shake you.



Preferred: hands-on experience with RentRedi, OwnerRez, or ClickUp; real estate or hospitality background; built or used Zapier / Make / n8n / custom GPTs.

What you'll get



60-day performance review, raise eligible. Annual review thereafter.

Paid time off after 90 days.

Software stipend — we cover ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro, Zapier, etc.

Direct line to the owner. Stable, profitable company. Long-term seat.



Apply with

(1) Your resume. (2) A 2-minute video: who you are, where you're based, why this role excites you, and one concrete story of making operations more efficient — challenge, action, outcome. (3) Bonus: share how you use AI today (custom Claude Project link, screenshot, Loom — anything specific).

We move fast on strong applications.

title: Inside Sales Agent Real Estate/ Cold Caller / Full time
description:
We are looking for a motivated and results-driven Inside Sales Agent (ISA) to join our growing real estate team. The Inside Sales Agent will be responsible for managing inbound and outbound leads, nurturing prospects, and setting qualified appointments for our real estate agents.



The ideal candidate is confident on the phone, highly organized, and skilled at building rapport with homeowners and buyers. This role focuses on lead conversion, appointment setting, and CRM management to help drive sales growth for the team.



Key Responsibilities:



Lead Management



Respond to and manage inbound leads from online sources, marketing campaigns, and referrals.



Make outbound calls to nurture leads, including past clients, sphere of influence, internet leads, FSBOs, expired listings, and cold prospects.



Qualify buyer and seller leads through structured discovery conversations.



Identify client needs, motivation, timeline, and price range.



Appointment Setting



Set qualified buyer and seller appointments for real estate agents.



Confirm appointments and ensure prospects are prepared for meetings.



Follow up with leads until they are ready to buy or sell.



Follow-Up & Nurturing



Execute consistent follow-up via calls, text messages, and
s.



Place leads into appropriate nurture campaigns and maintain consistent communication.



Re-engage cold or long-term leads.



CRM Management



Update and maintain accurate lead records in the company CRM.



Track communication, appointment status, and lead progression.



Tag leads appropriately and ensure proper pipeline management.



Lead Conversion Tracking



Monitor and report key metrics such as:



Calls made



Contacts made



Appointments set



Appointments showed



Maintain accountability for follow-up and conversion activities.

This position is offered on an independent contractor basis and is structured as an ongoing, long-term role for the right person. The focus is on consistency, performance, and reliability rather than short-term project work.

title: Full-Time Bookkeeper / Accountant (Accrual, QuickBooks Online)
description:
Applications without a resume link will not be reviewed.



Read Carefully Before Applying



This role requires real accrual-based accounting experience.



If your background is primarily cash-basis bookkeeping or transaction categorization without understanding balance sheet flow, this role is not a fit.



Applications that do not follow the instructions below will not be reviewed.



Non-Negotiable Requirements



You must already have hands-on experience with:



• QuickBooks Online (daily, ongoing use)

• Accrual-based accounting (applied, not theoretical)

• Recording and reviewing payroll journal entries (wages, taxes, accruals)

• Monthly bank and credit card reconciliations

• Supporting multiple client accounts

• Understanding how balance sheet accuracy directly impacts the P&L and financial decisions



If these are not already part of your work history, please do not apply.



About the Role



We are a U.S.-based fractional CFO firm supporting service-based businesses across the United States.



Our clients depend on clean books, accurate financials, and reliable reporting. This role supports CFO-level work. Accuracy matters more than speed.



You are not replacing a CFO. Your work supports CFO review, analysis, and decision-making. This is a tactical, detail-focused accounting role for someone who values structure, clarity, and getting things right.



This is a long-term, full-time position.



Core Responsibilities



Bookkeeping & Accounting



• Accurately categorize bank and credit card transactions in QuickBooks Online

• Perform monthly bank and credit card reconciliations

• Record and review payroll journal entries

• Ensure proper classification between COGS and operating expenses

• Maintain clean and consistent chart-of-accounts usage

• Proactively identify and flag anomalies or unclear transactions



Client & Process Support



• Learn each client’s accounting setup and nuances

• Ask clarifying questions when something does not make sense

• Create and maintain client-specific SOPs in Notion

• Improve documentation as processes evolve



CFO Support



• Prepare clean, reliable financials for CFO review

• Assist with month-end close

• Communicate issues clearly and concisely

• Take ownership of assigned clients and deadlines



Required Skills & Experience



• Strong QuickBooks Online experience

• Solid understanding of U.S. bookkeeping and payroll

• Deep, working knowledge of accrual-based accounting

• High attention to detail and accuracy

• Strong written and verbal English communication

• Able to explain how and why transactions are recorded

• Comfortable following structure while thinking independently

• Experience supporting multiple client accounts



Bonus (Not Required)



• Light budgeting or forecasting exposure

• Prior experience supporting CFOs or CPAs

• Experience preparing firm-ready financials



Education



• Accounting degree or related field strongly preferred



Tools You’ll Use



• QuickBooks Online

• Gusto or similar payroll platforms

• Notion

• Google Workspace

• Slack or similar communication tools



Work Schedule & Requirements



• Full-time, Monday through Friday

• Must be available during U.S. business hours through 2:00 PM Pacific / 5:00 PM Eastern

• Stable, high-speed, dedicated internet connection

• Backup internet or power source preferred

• Quiet, professional home office

• Strong responsiveness during working hours



Internet stability and availability during U.S. business hours will be verified during onboarding.



Growth Opportunity



This role can grow over time through increased client responsibility, deeper process ownership, and expanded accounting oversight as skills develop.



How to Apply (Required)



To be considered, you must submit all of the following:



• A link to your resume (Google Drive or Dropbox)

• Written answers to the questions below

• Confirmation that you can work the required U.S. hours consistently



Applications missing any item will be rejected automatically.



Written Questions (Answer Briefly, Bullet Points Only)



A customer prepays $12,000 in December for services delivered evenly from January through March.

• What account is credited when the cash is received?

• What balance appears on the balance sheet at December 31?



In QuickBooks Online, why does Undeposited Funds sometimes grow over time, and what does that usually indicate?



Payroll is processed in March but paid in April.

• When should payroll expense be recorded under accrual accounting?



Do not use outside tools. Short, clear answers only.



Final Note



This role is for someone who already understands accrual accounting and balance sheet flow. Training will be provided on our processes, not on accounting fundamentals.

title: Customer Support + eCommerce VA
description:
# Experienced E-Commerce Customer Support VA — Spiritual Apparel Brand (Long-Term, Part-Time ? Growth)

 

## ABOUT US

 

We are a fast-growing online apparel brand in the spiritual and mindful-living space. Our customers are warm, thoughtful women on personal growth journeys, and we take pride in giving them a customer experience that feels personal, kind, and human.

 

We are a small, focused team that moves quickly. We invest heavily in the people we hire, give them real ownership, and expect them to grow.

 

## WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 

This role is **not** for someone who needs to be told what to do every day. It is for someone who:

 

- **Is a true self-starter** — sees what needs doing and does it

- **Has obsessive attention to detail** — typos, inconsistencies, and missed steps bother you

- **Asks questions early and often** — you'd rather ask than guess

- **Welcomes daily communication** — you're responsive, available, and not hard to reach during your shift

- **Is highly organized** — you keep clean systems, not piles

- **Wants to grow beyond customer support** — eventually into product uploads, ad operations, design coordination, and more

- **Learns fast** — new tools don't intimidate you

- **Speaks and writes excellent English** — your written word represents our brand

- **Has strong work ethic and integrity** — you do the right thing when no one is watching

If three or more of those don't sound like you, please don't apply. We'd rather find the right person slowly than hire the wrong one quickly.

 

## RESPONSIBILITIES

 

**Primary:**

- Respond to customer
s through our helpdesk with warmth, clarity, and professionalism

- Handle tracking and shipping inquiries

- Coordinate print-on-demand order issues, replacements, and reprints in Printify

- Process refunds and store credits according to our policy

**Growth path (over the first 60–90 days, as you prove yourself):**

- Product uploads and listing management in Shopify and Printify

- Review management (Loox / Shopify reviews)

- Social media engagement (
 and comments on
 and Instagram)

- Light admin and reporting (spreadsheets, issue logs, KPI tracking)

- Eventually: ad operations support, design coordination, broader ops work

We provide custom AI tools and detailed response templates that handle most routine CS scenarios. Your job is to apply judgment, escalate the tricky stuff, and keep our customers feeling genuinely cared for.

 

## REQUIRED EXPERIENCE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

 

Applications without all four of these will not be considered:

 

1. **Hands-on Shopify experience** (orders, refunds, customer profiles, basic product management)

2. **Hands-on Printify experience** (or another print-on-demand platform — Printful, Gelato, etc.)

3. **At least 1 year of e-commerce customer support experience** with a real brand

4. **Strong written English** that does not need editing before sending to customers



## TOOLS WE USE

 

- Commslayer (helpdesk — training provided)

- Shopify

- Printify

- Gmail

- Slack (internal chat — daily communication expected)

- Google Drive / Sheets

- Loox (reviews)

- Meta Business Suite /
 / Instagram

- Canva (occasional)



## REQUIREMENTS

 

- Stable internet connection (speed test required)

- Unlimited data plan

- Laptop or desktop with reliable webcam and microphone

- Available within Philippine daytime hours with the potential of **2–4 hours of overlap with US Central Time** daily

- Looking for long-term, stable work (not project-based)



## SCHEDULE & COMPENSATION

 

- **Hours:** 20–25 hours/week to start, with potential to grow to full-time

- **Pay:** ?25,000–?30,000 PHP/month (depending on experience). This is firm.

- **Payment Method:** Paid via Wise. Weekly during the first 8 weeks (to build trust on both sides), then biweekly thereafter.

- **Employment Type:** Long-term part-time, with a clear growth path to full time

- **13th Month Pay:** Provided after 6 months of service (prorated based on tenure)

- **Training:** Paid training during onboarding

- **Raises:** Performance-based, reviewed at 6 months and annually



## HOW TO APPLY

 

Please submit your application as a single OnlineJobs.ph message. **Read the entire post before applying.**

 

### A) BASIC INFO

- Full name and city/province

- Internet speed test screenshot

- Desired monthly salary (PHP)

- Link to LinkedIn or portfolio (if applicable)



### B) WORKLOAD & AVAILABILITY (be honest — we will verify)

- Are you currently working with other clients or employers? List each: type of work, weekly hours, how long you've been with them.

- What is your **realistic** weekly capacity for us, given your other commitments?

- What hours can you reliably work that overlap 2–4 hours with US Central Time?

- If this role grew over time, what is your maximum weekly availability?



### C) EXPERIENCE QUESTIONS

 

Please answer each in 3–6 sentences. Be specific. We can tell when answers are vague or generic.

 

1. **Shopify experience.** Walk us through your most relevant Shopify role. What store? What were your specific responsibilities? What did you own end-to-end?

2. **Printify (or POD) experience.** What platform, what brand, what tasks? Have you handled order issues, reprints, variant management, or product uploads? Give a specific example.

3. **Hardest customer support situation you've handled.** What was the issue, what did you do, and what was the outcome? We want details.

4. **Self-starter example.** Tell us about a time you saw a problem at work and fixed it without being asked. Be specific about what you noticed and what you did.

5. **Time you were stuck.** Describe a recent task where you were unsure how to proceed. What did you do? Who did you ask, and how?



### D) FIT QUESTIONS

 

1. Why this role specifically? What about it appeals to you beyond it being a job? (We're not looking for flattery — we're looking for genuine fit.)

2. What would make you leave a job like this one? (Honest answers only — there are no wrong answers, but there are unbelievable ones.)

3. Where do you want to be in 2 years professionally? Be specific.



### E) CUSTOMER SCENARIO

 

A customer
s:

 

> *"I ordered the shirt in size M based on your size chart and it's WAY too small. I'm 5'4", 140lbs and the chart said M would fit. I'm so frustrated. I want a full refund AND I want you to know your sizing is misleading."*

 

Write the
 reply you'd send. (4–7 sentences. Don't promise anything outside standard policy — but show us your judgment, tone, and how you'd de-escalate.)

 

### F) DOCUMENTS

 

Attach your CV or resume.

 

### G) ATTENTION TO DETAIL

 

Include the word **"lightkeeper"** as the first word of your application, but not in the subject line. Applications missing this word are deleted unread.

 

## NEXT STEPS

 

We review every application that follows the instructions above. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a one-way video interview (recorded on your own time). Final candidates will have a paid trial week before a long-term offer.

 

## FINAL NOTE

 

We're respectful, direct, and genuinely invested in the success of the people we hire. If you want a long-term home where you can grow, where good work is noticed, and where you'll be treated like a real teammate — we'd love to hear from you.

 

If you're looking for a job to coast in, this isn't it.

title: Phone & Outreach Specialist –  Remote

This job IS for you if:



-  You can speak clearly and confidently in English

   (be specific — no generic answers)

Voice Recording Instructions (VERY IMPORTANT):

I’m looking for someone long-term.



This is a serious opportunity for someone who performs well. There is clear potential for immediate increased hours, greater responsibility, and long-term growth.

title: Marketing VA – Social Media, Content and Ads
description:

Bottomless brunches | Trivia nights | Themed pop-ups

Ticketed events and interactive experiences

Experience is a must. READ APPLICATION INSTRUCTION!! We will not respond to hire me if you do not send resume & portfolio!



Our brand is fun, social, a little unhinged (in the best way), and built around bringing people together. From viral brunch 
nts to packed themed events, we focus on creating an atmosphere people want to be part of and come back to.

We are not a quiet restaurant brand. We are a social, experience-driven business, and our marketing reflects that.



*If you enjoy working on brands that are bold, fast-paced, and constantly evolving, you will thrive here. If your experience is limited to posting content without understanding campaigns, paid ads, or audience behavior, this role is not the right fit.

Driving engagement and attendance

“Bridgerton Pop-Up Bar” and turn it into:



scroll-stopping content | consistent visibility | targeted campaigns| real turnout



**For faster hiring & to Apply - READ till the end of job listing and submit ALL of the following: using this link, then send us a message letting us know it is complete.







What You’ll Do



Campaign Execution

, TikTok, and LinkedIn

 | Instagram| TikTok | LinkedIn

What Success Looks Like

Increased brand awareness and engagement



Requirements



Technical & Tools

Meta & Ads Manager

TikTok 

LinkedIn is a bonus 

Experience with
 marketing platforms and automation tools

Work Style & Expectations

Important

title: Catering Sales Manager (Full-Time) $1200-1400
description:
The Point Restaurant Group + Blind Tiger Catering 

READ TO THE BOTTOM for Application Process



About Us

The Point Restaurant Group is a growing hospitality company specializing in high-volume events, catering, and experience-driven dining. 



We operate two distinct brands:

The Point – in-house restaurant catering and private events

Blind Tiger Catering – full-service off-site catering, food, beverage, and mobile bar experiences



We handle everything from small gatherings to large-scale weddings and corporate events, with a strong focus on efficiency, organization, and conversion. Our systems, automations, and processes are already built. We are now focused on scaling bookings, improving conversion, and delivering a seamless client experience from inquiry to execution.



Position Overview

This is a full-time, sales-focused role responsible for converting inquiries into booked events while delivering a seamless, high-touch client experience.



You will manage the full lifecycle:

Inquiry > Qualification > Proposal > Booking > Planning > 14-Day Handoff



We already have systems, automations, and workflows in place. Your role is to execute within those systems, manage the pipeline, and drive revenue.



This is not an entry-level position.

We are looking for someone with prior experience in catering/hospitality sales, client communication, or event/catering coordination who can take ownership and work independently.



Key Responsibilities

Sales & Booking (Primary Focus)

Manage all inbound catering and event inquiries 
, website, CRM, social)

Qualify leads based on event type, guest count, budget, and timeline

Send proposals, menus, and contracts through CRM

Follow up consistently with warm and cold leads

Convert inquiries into booked events

Upsell enhancements and higher-value packages



Client Experience & Event Planning

Serve as the main point of contact from inquiry through handoff

Guide clients through menu selection and event structure

Answer questions and provide recommendations confidently

Maintain a professional, polished, and on-brand tone

Keep clients engaged and moving toward booking



CRM, Automations & Workflow Management

Manage all leads and communication through Dubsado CRM

Utilize existing workflows, canned
s, and automations

Understand when to use automation vs when to personalize communication

Make minor updates to workflows, templates, and automation triggers as needed

Ensure all leads move through the pipeline with no missed follow-ups

Maintain accurate tags, notes, and statuses



Pipeline & Project Management

Track all leads, bookings, and event progress in 

Move events through each stage of the pipeline

Input booked events into internal templates and manage pre-event tasks

Ensure timelines and internal deliverables are completed



Event Documentation & Prep

Create and manage:

BEOs (Banquet Event Orders)

Event timelines and internal notes

Client-facing menus (Canva)

Ensure all details are finalized prior to handoff



Handoff to Operations

Transition events to the execution team 14 days prior to event

Ensure all details are complete, accurate, and ready for execution



Tools & Systems

Dubsado (CRM, workflows, proposals, contracts)


 (lead tracking and project management)

Canva

Google Workspace

Social Media to scan groups for leads

 Blast



Compensation

$1,200-$1,400 /month base salary based on experience in this industry



Performance-based bonuses:

$10 = $0–$2,999 events

$20 = $3,000–$4,999 events

$30 = $5,000+ events

Bonus is paid on signed contracts with deposit received.

We average 5-25 events per month 



Who You Are

Sales-driven and confident in closing leads

Strong written and verbal English communication

Highly organized and detail-oriented

Able to manage multiple events and timelines simultaneously

Proactive with follow-ups and pipeline ownership



You also:

Have experience working in a CRM system

Understand workflows and automations

Know when to personalize vs rely on templates

Can think beyond tasks and focus on results



Application Process (Required)

1) Message us with resume & portfolio



2) To be considered, you must submit a 2–3 minute video answering the following:

How would you follow up with a lead that has not responded after receiving a quote?

How would you upsell a client who only inquired about a lower-budget event?

When would you choose not to use an automated response and instead send a custo
ssage?



Final Note

This role is responsible for managing and converting leads into booked events. Please include any relevant experience or results related to sales, client management, or event coordination. This is not an entry-level position.

Candidates MUST have prior experience in catering/hospitality sales, client communication, or event/catering coordination who can take ownership and work independently.

title: Creative, Playful UGC Video Editor
description:
Agency: Ostrich — US-based UGC Ad Agency

Start Date: ASAP

Location: Remote (must be available 3-4 hrs a day on UK time)



Are you the kind of video editor who notices the little things? Do you love turning raw, personality-packed footage into scroll-stopping content that feels native to TikTok and Instagram? If you’re playful, creatively obsessive, and deeply tuned into current UGC trends, we want you on our team.



Ostrich is a fast-growing ad agency that specializes in User Generated Content (UGC) for paid social. We create engaging, authentic-feeling video ads for a wide range of brands. Think lo-fi, high-converting, human-first content that blends seamlessly into social feeds.



We're looking for a full-time UGC Video Editor who gets the vibe. That means:

- Not overproduced.

- No stock footage.

- Just clever editing, relatable 
nts, and platform-native creativity.



What You’ll Be Doing:

- Editing 30–60 short-form videos/month (30–90 seconds each) using creator-shot raw footage

- Adding playful overlays, punchy captions, and smart cuts that mirror platform trends

- Collaborating closely with a creative lead based in the UK (2 days/week overlap required)

- Making fast, thoughtful revisions based on feedback

- Staying current on TikTok/IG trends, audio 
s, and native transitions



You’re a Great Fit If You:

- Are a wizard in Adobe Premiere Pro

- Speak and write excellent English

- Are obsessed with details, captions, and clever pacing

- Know how to use in-camera transitions like snaps, covers, and taps (and splice them cleanly)

- Never rely on stock footage

- Love being part of a collaborative, feedback-driven creative process

- Follow UGC creators and brands just for fun

- Have a knack for solving little editing problems before they become big ones



What We Want to See From You:

- Your best UGC-style edits only (no reels with stock footage, motion graphics templates, or polished brand films)

- Bonus points for examples using TikTok-native transitions (spliced, not effects-added)

- Any videos where your editing made the content funnier, clearer, or more relatable

title: Property Manager (Long-term career with paid holiday) (BPO or job-hopping VA's should not apply)
description:
Due to a period of development and growth LifeStyle are now looking to recruit a number of experienced Administrators and particularly those that have excellent client communication skills to join their dedicated and close-nit team.



Who are we:



LifeStyle are probably North Bristol's most active Letting & Estate Agents. We have been serving the local area for over 30 years and have established relationships with clients, partners and tenants.



We take care of over 1000 Landlords with approximately 3000 units that result in a fair number of daily rent payments that require processing.



We pride ourselves on having family roots and our staff turnover is extremely low. We are a team. We work hard, respect each other and enjoy the journey - together!



Responsibilities of the role:



Control the day to day elements of the property referencing process - ensuring prospective tenants are eligible for the property they wish to rent.



Liaising with tenants and landlords.



Operating different cloud based systems to ensure tenants are legal, eligible and insured for the property they wish to let.



You will be timely and not afraid of dealing with clients on the telephone or
.



You will be working to constant deadlines to ensure people can move in to their property.



What we are looking for:



Confident and client focused, clear speaking professional that is able to work at pace in a fast moving environment and to deadline.

An accomplished team player, who has a strong work ethic and wants to do a great job!

Highly organised with the ability to prioritise and multi task.

Meticulous and accurate with impeccable attention to detail.

Confident communicator with a good telephone manner.

Building relationships with people internally and externally with come naturally to you.

Most importantly - our roots are in a family run business. It is the core of LifeStyle.

We operate morally, ethically and lovingly as one big family with the respect and care for each other and our clients.



We are always on the look out for like-minded individuals. We believe it's what makes us who we are !



PLEASE NOTE:

There is a lot of Time-Wasters and people that try to get jobs or multiple jobs on Online Jobs. So we feel the need to clarify.



We have OVER 20 years experience hiring remotely - and the entry in this business begins at (GBP) £550 + £15 health benefit and 56 hours paid holiday. We pay in GBP.



We are also very aware that many rates on here are AGENCY rates. Typically the rates advertised by VA's is way off and it is people trying to be paid Agency rates via Direct to Consumer. These rates reflect US shift times. In BPO agency the standard pay is about $200.



Entry in this business begins at (GBP) £550 + £15 health benefit and 56 hours paid holiday. We pay in GBP.

That is approx 46,000 pesos.



This role is UK time (approx 4pm Philippine time) - Not the USA Graveyard shift, they destroys mental health etc.



This is a career with stability and team-value and not a VA/BPO job.

title: Remote Case Manager for US Law Firm (Personal Injury & Criminal)
description:
Our established US law firm is seeking a highly skilled and experienced Remote Case Manager to manage a dynamic caseload spanning Personal Injury and Criminal Law. This role is the central hub for our clients' cases, ensuring every detail is tracked and every deadline is met during critical times in their lives.



This position starts as part-time, but we are specifically looking for a candidate who can grow into a full-time, key member of our team based on performance. If you have deep experience in these fast-paced legal areas and want a long-term career opportunity, we want to hear from you.



Key Responsibilities:



Core Case Management:



· Personal Injury: Conduct diligent follow-up on medical records with hospitals and providers; draft client correspondence; open and submit insurance claims; prepare medical record packets.

· Criminal Law: Assist with managing case milestones; track court dates and filing deadlines; handle client status updates.

· Draft and send critical correspondence (exit letters, updates) to clients and providers across all case types.

· Communicate with clients via text and
 to provide support and gather information.



CRM, Calendaring & Administrative Precision:



· Log all incoming
s and faxes into our system, ensuring no detail is missed.

· Upload attachments and update task statuses for the Case Manager in our CRM.

· Print/Save
s as PDF and copy the full text into Clio as a note with a descriptive subject line.

· Manage the firm calendar: Accurately enter all deadlines, court dates, hearings, and appointments.



Must-Have Qualifications (Non-Negotiable):



· Proven Experience in US Law: Minimum of 2+ years as a Case Manager, Legal Assistant, or Paralegal for a US-based law firm. Direct experience with Personal Injury AND/OR Criminal law is required.

· Software Proficiency: Must be highly proficient with Clio Manage. Experience with calendaring critical deadlines is essential.

· Impeccable English: Exceptional written and spoken English is a strict requirement for direct, sensitive communication with US clients, courts, and providers.

· Detail-Oriented & Organized: Ability to manage multiple tasks and urgent deadlines without letting anything slip through the cracks.

· Long-Term Potential: We are seeking a career-oriented professional interested in growing with our firm.



How to Apply (Please Read Carefully):



To be considered, please follow these two steps. Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.



1. Click "Apply" on this post.

2. In your application message, please include:

· A brief introduction about yourself and why you are a great fit for this specific role.

· Confirm your experience with US law firms, specifying your background in Personal Injury and/or Criminal law and the state(s) you have experience with.

· Confirm your proficiency with Clio.

· To prove you have read this entire post, please begin your message with the phrase: "Precision in PI & Criminal Law."



We are excited to find a long-term partner to join our team. We thank all applicants for their interest, but only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

title: Full-Stack Web Developer
description:
Savant Marketing is the #1 marketing agency for landscapers. We’re looking for a highly detail-oriented and design-obsessed Full Stack Web Developer who can help us design, build, and manage high-performing websites for our clients.



This is an excellent role for someone who takes pride in precision, cares deeply about how things look and feel, and wants a long-term, stable position at a fast-growing digital marketing agency.



Role Overview:



We’re searching for a Full Stack Web Developer who operates with a high level of focus, consistency, and attention to detail—and can consistently deliver websites that are not only functional, but visually exceptional.



This role is perfect for someone who:



-Notices when spacing is off by a few pixels

-Cares deeply about alignment, typography, and visual hierarchy

-Takes ownership of their work and holds themselves to a high standard

-Prefers focused, independent work and getting things done right the first time

-Finds satisfaction in clean, polished, and well-structured design



You will work closely with our operational and client success teams to create and manage websites for landscaping and outdoor living businesses.



Key Responsibilities:



-Design and develop high-converting websites for clients in the landscaping and outdoor living industry.

-Build responsive, mobile-first websites with a strong focus on user experience, flow, and conversion.

-Handle both front-end (UI/UX, layout, design) and back-end (functionality, integrations) development.

-Ensure every website is visually polished, balanced, and aligned down to the smallest detail (spacing, padding, typography, responsiveness).

-Optimize websites for speed, SEO, and overall performance.

-Set up and manage Google Analytics across all websites.

-Integrate websites with CRMs, forms, tracking systems, and automation platforms.

-Maintain, update, and troubleshoot existing client websites.

-Collaborate with the marketing team to ensure websites align with ad strategy and lead generation goals.

-Organize code, assets, and systems for clarity, structure, and long-term scalability.

-Continuously refine and improve design quality by studying high-performing websites and modern design trends.



Requirements:



-3+ years experience in full stack web development.

-Strong front-end development and design skills (this is a priority).

-A sharp eye for design, layout, spacing, and overall visual experience.

-Experience building clean, modern, and high-converting websites.

-Proficiency in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and modern frameworks.

-Experience with CMS platforms (WordPress, Webflow).

-Experience setting up and managing Google Analytics.

-Experience with backend development (APIs, databases, integrations).

-Understanding of SEO best practices and website performance optimization.

-Highly detail-oriented with a commitment to delivering polished, error-free work.

title: MARKETING SPECIALIST
description:
Position Overview:



We are seeking a dynamic and skilled Marketing Specialist to help transform our marketing team into a highly efficient,

system-driven engine. This role will focus on connecting our products with real-world customer needs by modularizing them

into reusable building blocks, enabling customer self-selection through structured scenarios, cases, and demand collection

strategies.



As a Marketing Specialist, you will play a key role in reshaping Buck Sports from a “custom apparel supplier” to a

“decision-assist platform” that drives customer decisions and ensures long-term growth.



Responsibilities:



Develop and implement a system for modularizing products into reusable building blocks (e.g., fabric, craft, fit, MOQ,

lead-time tiers).

Design and manage a scenario system that helps structure customer needs based on real-world usage contexts.

Build and structure case studies that are reusable by both sales teams and our website, with clear decision logic and

outcomes.

Create and implement demand collection strategies that capture relevant customer data and map it to product modules,

scenarios, and cases.

Facilitate inter-department collaboration to ensure smooth integration of marketing systems across the organization.

Contribute to transforming Buck Sports into a “decision-assist platform” through innovative marketing systems.

Analyze data and provide insights for improving marketing campaigns and customer targeting, ensuring that decisions

are backed by robust reporting and analytics.

Manage social media platforms and create engaging content to promote products and engage customers.

Assist with website design and development, ensuring a seamless user experience and clear product presentation.

Create and execute
 marketing campaigns using Mailchimp to drive engagement and conversions.

Use Canva for designing and editing visual content for both digital and print materials.

Edit photos and videos for marketing materials and social media promotion.

Be knowledgeable with translation tools and ensure content is accurately localized for diverse audiences.

Create video content, including editing, as well as developing training materials to ensure proper usage of systems and

platforms across the organization.



Requirements:



Proven experience in system-driven marketing, with expertise in scenario planning or product modularization.

Strong background in data-driven marketing and customer self-selection strategies.

Background in reporting and analytics, with the ability to analyze and present actionable insights.

Expertise in social media management, with the ability to create engaging content across platforms.

Knowledge of website design, user experience, and the ability to assist in website management.

Experience using tools like Mailchimp for
 campaigns, Canva for graphic design/editing, and social media

platforms for promotion.

Proven experience in video and photo editing for marketing purposes.

Familiarity with translation tools and experience ensuring content is accurately localized.

Experience in catalog creation and management.

Ability to think structurally and strategically, beyond just creativity.

Excellent organizational skills with a keen attention to detail.

Capable of working independently with strong initiative and the ability to prioritize tasks effectively.

Excellent collaboration skills to work cross-functionally with Product, Sales, and IT teams.



If you’re excited about transforming marketing into a strategic powerhouse and have the skills to drive customer decision

making, we would love to hear from you

title: Competitor Research Specialist - Women Fashion Dropshipping
description:
We are a growing women's fashion dropshipping brand and we are looking for a sharp, detail-oriented researcher who lives and breathes the Meta Ad Library. Someone who has done this work at a high level — for stores that mean business — and can bring that same precision and consistency to our team every single day.



What you will do

Conduct daily competitor research using the Meta Ad Library exclusively — this is the only tool you will use.



Follow a daily country schedule — each day you rotate through a set list of countries, systematically scanning which stores are actively running ads in the women's fashion niche.



Track and log competitor stores, focusing on how many active ads they are running and whether they meet our internal benchmark — you will receive a clear framework for this.



Report your findings daily in a structured format — clean, consistent, and ready to act on. No interpretation needed, just accurate data.



Deliver consistent output 7 days a week, 1–2 hours per day. This role requires reliability above all else.



Use an extensive keyword set to search the Meta Ad Library effectively — you know which terms surface the right stores and how to cast a wide net across different markets.



Who we are looking for

We are not looking for someone who has "tried" competitor research. We want an A-player — a true specialist who has done this work at a serious level, for stores that were already operating at scale.



You have hands-on experience doing competitor research for e-commerce stores generating $50,000+ per month in revenue — this is non-negotiable. We are not your first rodeo.



You are deeply familiar with the Meta Ad Library and know how to use it to map out which stores are actively spending and at what scale.



You have researched multiple countries and know how to navigate different markets in the women's fashion niche using the right search terms.



You are self-driven, take initiative, and do not need to be micromanaged. You follow the process precisely and flag anything worth noting without being asked.



You are consistent, punctual, and treat daily research with the seriousness of a professional — every day counts, no exceptions.



To show us you have read this job post in full, start your application with the word banana. Applications that do not include this word will not be reviewed.



Growth potential

This role is built for someone who wants to grow. If you perform well and show initiative, there is a real opportunity to expand your responsibilities within the business — whether that is in product sourcing, ad strategy support, or a broader research role. We invest in people who invest in us.



How to apply

In your application, tell us about your experience with competitor research — which niches you have covered, how many countries you have worked across, and what your daily process looks like. Tell us about the stores you have worked for and what their monthly revenue was.



Also describe your keyword approach: how large is your keyword list, and how do you build and maintain it across different markets?

title: Medical Billing Specialist
description:
ST. ELIZABETH MANAGEMENT GROUP

Revenue Cycle Management • Position Description



RCM Billing Specialist – Virtual Assistant (Philippines)



Position Title RCM Billing Specialist – Virtual Assistant

Employment Type Full-Time, Remote (Philippines-based)

Reporting To SEMG RCM Team Lead

Hours Monday – Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST (Houston time zone)

Compensation Competitive; commensurate with experience and qualifications

English Requirement C2 Proficiency (CEFR) – mandatory, non-negotiable

Target Start Upon SEMG RCM Team Lead hire and onboarding (est. 30–60 days)





About St. Elizabeth Management Group

St. Elizabeth Management Group (SEMG) is a Houston-area healthcare management company overseeing revenue cycle operations across a portfolio of physician groups and specialty practices. SEMG is currently executing a strategic consolidation of billing operations — absorbing functions previously distributed across multiple external vendors and entity-level staff into a centralized, high-performance internal RCM team.



The entities within scope include a primary care / ACO-affiliated physician group, a telehealth platform delivering Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM), Chronic Care Management (CCM), and Principal Care Management (PCM), and a multi-site urgent care group with active Workers’ Compensation billing operations. These are not startup practices — combined gross monthly charges exceed $400,000, and this team will be accountable for protecting and improving that revenue.





Role Overview

SEMG is hiring two RCM Billing Specialists to serve as the operational core of its newly centralized revenue cycle team. These are not administrative assistant roles. You will execute hands-on medical billing across multiple EHR systems and payer portals, manage denial workflows, support claims submission and AR follow-up, and operate with a high degree of accuracy and autonomy under the direction of the SEMG RCM Team Lead.



You will work with real patients’ claims, real payer timelines, and real financial consequences. The team being built is small, which means individual performance is visible and impactful. Candidates who thrive in high-accountability, detail-intensive environments with clear metrics will succeed here. Candidates who need significant hand-holding or who treat billing as a data-entry function will not.





Core Responsibilities

Claims Submission & Charge Entry

• Review and submit claims across multiple EHR platforms (Athena, eClinicalWorks, Agility)

• Verify charge capture accuracy, including CPT/ICD-10 code pairing and modifier application

• Apply correct Place of Service (POS) codes based on service delivery context (critical for RPM/CCM billing)

• Confirm patient eligibility and insurance verification prior to claim submission

• Identify and correct coding errors prior to initial submission to minimize first-pass denials



Denial Management & Appeals

• Monitor payer remittances (ERAs) and identify claim denials by reason code

• Categorize and track denials by payer, code, and denial type in designated tracking systems

• Draft and submit written appeals with supporting clinical or coding documentation

• Conduct follow-up calls to payer provider relations lines when written appeals are insufficient

• Escalate complex or high-dollar denials to the RCM Team Lead with a documented summary



Accounts Receivable Follow-Up

• Work AR aging queues on a defined weekly cycle; prioritize by dollar value and timely filing deadlines

• Contact payers via portal and telephone to resolve claims in pending, processing, or suspended status

• Identify patterns in delayed or underpaid claims and report to Team Lead with supporting data

• Document all follow-up activity with date, contact name, and outcome in the billing system



Workers’ Compensation Billing Support

• Submit WC claims to employer and TPA payers per applicable state fee schedule requirements

• Support employer invoicing workflows including charge reconciliation against spreadsheet-tracked accounts

• Monitor WC claim status and follow up with adjusters as directed by Team Lead

• Maintain clean separation of WC AR from commercial/Medicare AR in reporting



RPM / CCM / PCM Billing Compliance

• Apply correct CPT codes for Remote Patient Monitoring (99453, 99454, 99457, 99458) and Chronic Care Management (99490, 99491) services

• Verify that time logs and device data thresholds meet billing criteria before claim submission

• Monitor for POS and modifier combinations that affect reimbursement on telehealth-delivered services

• Flag any RPM/CCM claims that fail enrollment or documentation criteria prior to submission



Reporting & Documentation

• Maintain accurate records of claim status, denial activity, and follow-up in the RCM tracking system

• Provide weekly AR aging summaries to Team Lead in the required format

• Document payer-specific billing rules and exceptions as discovered, for team reference

• Participate in regular team calls with the SEMG RCM Team Lead (video, CST hours)





Requirements & Qualifications



Requirement Type Notes

C2 English Proficiency (CEFR) Required Must be able to draft written payer appeals and conduct verbal follow-up calls without language barriers

Minimum 3 years U.S. medical billing experience Required Must be U.S. payer system, not international

Denial management & appeals experience Required Passive claim monitoring is not sufficient; must have active appeal experience

CPT / ICD-10 coding knowledge Required Applied coding accuracy, not general familiarity

ERA / EOP reading & reconciliation Required Must be able to interpret and act on remittance data without guidance

Experience with Athena, eClinicalWorks, or Agility EHR Preferred Any one of the three is sufficient; all three is a strong advantage

RPM / CCM / PCM billing experience Preferred Particularly codes 99453, 99454, 99457, 99490

Workers’ Compensation billing experience Preferred Texas WC system preferred; any U.S. WC experience considered

Multi-payer environment experience (Medicare, Medicaid, MA plans, Commercial) Required Single-payer background is insufficient for this role

Professional certification (CPC, CPB, CBCS, or equivalent) Preferred Not required; strong practical track record may substitute

Reliable high-speed internet and professional home office setup Required Video calls and EHR portal access are daily requirements

Availability during CST business hours (8 AM – 5 PM, M–F) Required No overlap exceptions; this is a synchronous team





Who You Are

You hold yourself to a standard that most people would find exhausting. You notice a denial reason code that doesn’t match the clinical documentation, and you investigate rather than accept it. You know the difference between a denial you can appeal and one that reflects a billing error on your end, and you treat them differently. You don’t let AR sit because a payer is slow — you call them.



You are comfortable working independently across multiple EHR systems without someone watching over your shoulder. You understand that in a small, centralized team, your accuracy directly affects physician cash flow. That fact motivates you rather than intimidates you.



Your English is strong enough to write an appeal letter that reads like it was written by a U.S. biller, because payer reviewers read hundreds of appeals and notice the ones that are vague or awkward. You are precise in writing, organized in your AR queues, and direct in your communication with your Team Lead.





What to Expect

This is a consolidation-phase hire. SEMG is building this team from scratch, which means processes, templates, and workflows will need to be developed alongside day-to-day billing operations. The first 90 days will require adaptability — you will be working with three different EHR systems, two or more payer portals, and billing profiles that have not always been well-managed.



You will report directly to the SEMG RCM Team Lead, who is also a new hire. Expect close collaboration as the team establishes its operating cadence. Performance expectations are real: collection rates, denial rates, and AR aging will be tracked from day one. This is not a role where performance is evaluated informally once a year.



On the other side of this ramp: you will be part of a lean, well-compensated team with direct visibility to leadership, clear metrics, and genuine upside in terms of role stability and growth as SEMG scales its managed entities. There is no bureaucratic layer between this team and decisions. If you see a billing problem, you report it and it gets addressed.





Performance Expectations



Metric 90-Day Target 12-Month Target

First-pass claim acceptance rate ?95% per assigned entity ?97?nial follow-up (within timely filing) 100% — no unworked denials 100% maintained

AR > 90 days (as % of total AR) Baseline established; trending down

This role requires a strong balance of visual design, user experience, and technical execution, with a focus on delivering a high-performing, conversion-optimized digital storefront.



 **Key Responsibilities**



1. Website Design & User Experience



* Design and deliver a high-end, visually compelling website aligned to luxury brand standards

* Develop intuitive, user-centric layouts across:



  * Homepage

  * Product detail pages

  * Collection/category pages

  * Landing pages

  * Ensure consistent application of brand guidelines, typography, and visual hierarchy



2. Development & Implementation



* Build, configure, and launch the website (Shopify or comparable e-commerce platform preferred)

* Ensure full mobile responsiveness and cross-browser compatibility

* Implement site architecture, navigation, and core functionality

* Integrate third-party tools (payments, analytics, CRM, marketing integrations)



3. E-Commerce Optimization



* Apply conversion rate optimization (CRO) best practices

* Optimize checkout flow, product merchandising, and on-site engagement

* Support implementation of upsell, cross-sell, and promotional features



4. Performance & Quality Assurance



* Ensure site performance meets standards for:

 * Load speed

  * Reliability

  * SEO readiness

  * Conduct QA testing prior to launch (functional, UX, and device testing)

  * Troubleshoot and resolve defects post-launch



5. Ongoing Support & Enhancements



* Provide post-launch support, updates, and enhancements as needed

* Collaborate with marketing and product teams on new features, campaigns, and content updates



**Required Qualifications**



* 3–7+ years of experience in website design and development with a focus on e-commerce

* Demonstrated experience building and launching Shopify (or similar) websites

* Strong portfolio showcasing:



  * Premium or luxury brand aesthetics

  * High-quality UX/UI design



 Proficiency in:

  * Shopify platform (themes, customization)

  * HTML, CSS (JavaScript preferred)

 * Working knowledge of: SEO principles & Conversion optimization strategies



**Preferred Qualifications**



* Experience working with **luxury, fashion, or lifestyle brands**

* Familiarity with:



  * Figma, Adobe Creative Suite, or similar design tools

* Experience integrating:



  * Payment gateways

  * Marketing automation tools

  * Analytics platforms (e.g., Google Analytics)



**Success Metrics**



* On-time, high-quality website launch

* Site performance (speed, uptime)

* Conversion rate and user engagement metrics

* Alignment with brand standards and user experience expectations



**Submission Requirements** PLEASE DO NOT APPLY IF YOU DO NOT HAVE THE EXPERIENCE



Candidates must provide:



* MUST INCLUDE THAT YOU HAVE READ THE REQUIREMENTS AND HAVE THE NECESSARY EXPERIENCE

* Portfolio of relevant work (live sites preferred)

* Summary of role in each project (design, development, or both)

* Availability and estimated timeline for delivery



* Candidates should demonstrate the ability to operate in a fast-paced, startup environment

* Strong communication skills and ability to incorporate feedback are critical

* Preference for candidates with both design and technical execution capabilities

title: Administrator / Admin Assistant
description:
At MBS Accountants, we are expanding our team due to continued growth. This is a rare opportunity to join a small, close-knit team in a leading innovative accountancy practice and play a fundamental role in helping business owners get a grip on their finances and make real leaps towards achieving their goals.



What We Are Offering

As well as becoming part of a leading innovative accountancy practice, you will also receive:



• Competitive salary – PHP 40,000 per month

• 40-hour working week

• 13th-month pay scheme

• PhilHealth during probation, with full health cover on successful completion

• Extra annual leave over the festive period – 2 weeks off over Christmas and New Year

• Clear steps for growth in your role and salary

• Quarterly input sessions on company direction – your voice genuinely counts

• Regular team get-togethers – online escape rooms, quizzes, and more

• Full training and support from both our Philippine and UK tea
mbers



What the Team Say



"I love the quarterly reviews as it gets everyone involved and all opinions are listened to" – Lauren, Senior Bookkeeper



"Everyone cares and is really helpful so you are never worried about asking questions" – Regina, Administrator



"We have an ability to have a say on our and the company's future – it's not just a job, it's about our personal life too" – Georgia, Client Manager



"I enjoy the work culture and environment as there is a right mix of professionalism and fun" – Gerald, Accountant



About the Role

You will be a highly organised, detail-driven administrator who takes pride in keeping everything accurate, accessible, and running smoothly. Your day-to-day responsibilities will include:



• Onboarding new clients into our systems

• Chasing clients, suppliers, and third parties for outstanding information

• Firm-wide AML (Anti-Money Laundering) compliance – you will own this

• Confirmation statement preparation and filing

• Director-only payroll processing

• Some video editing (training provided)

• Sending client-wide communications and
s

• Updating and maintaining our CRM and practice management systems

• Creating, amending, and improving internal processes



We are not expecting someone to join with the full range of skills from day one. MBS is a highly systemised and process-driven firm with a comprehensive set of tools and documentation to support you. You will be trained by both members of our existing Philippine team and our UK team, and you will meet one of our Philippine tea
mbers during the interview process.



A can-do attitude and strong organisational skills are essential. Everything else can be built.



About MBS Accountants

At MBS we do more than just accounting – we help business owners take control of their numbers, grow their profits, and build the lifestyle and freedom they want. Our approach combines smart technology, clear strategies, and straight-talking support to make a real difference for our clients.



We are a fast-growing, forward-thinking firm reshaping what modern accounting looks like. We use AI and the latest systems to stay ahead, and we are on an ambitious path of growth through innovation and strategic acquisitions.



For our team, this means being part of a company where:



• Everyone knows the bigger picture and where we are headed

• There is space to grow your skills and take on new challenges

• Professionalism and a fun, relaxed environment sit comfortably side by side

• Work-life balance is genuinely valued and supported



Our Values

We are looking for people who live by these principles:



Do What You Say

Nobody likes being let down. Understand the importance of each task, agree realistic timescales, and stick to them – or communicate early when challenges arise.



Open and Honest

Being a closed book does not help anyone. We believe life is just life – work and home are not two separate worlds. By being open and honest about your whole situation, we can be empathetic and work together towards growth and positivity.



Take Responsibility

Work-life balance is fundamental to a healthy life. We have core hours but each role carries key outcomes. Occasionally, flexibility will be needed to meet those outcomes – and taking responsibility to act accordingly is key.



Team Player

We support and empower each other. There will be times when you step outside your standard scope to help a colleague – not because it is expected, but because you know it is the right thing to do.



Respect

Respect is everything and should be a given – not because of someone's position, but simply because we are all human beings. Being candid with someone about their weaknesses and mistakes, when done with care, is an absolute sign of respect.



Is This Role Right for You?

If you can answer yes to all of the below, this role is perfect for you:



• Do you love being organised?

• Do you have a can-do attitude?

• Do you like data to be stored accurately?

• Do you like things to be easy to find?

• Do you question things that do not seem right?

• Do you ask questions to understand how you can make a greater impact?

• Do you enjoy challenges, and recognise when to ask for help and when to keep pushing?

• Are you committed to regular self-improvement and personal development?

• Do you like being recognised for the hard work you put in?



How to Apply

If this sounds like your next role, we would love to hear from you.



• Step 1 – Apply via OnlineJobs.ph

• Step 2 – Complete our skills test by copying and pasting the following into your browser = 
/adminrole

title: ### GoHighLevel Funnels Builder and Automations Expert | Immediate Start | Remote ###
description:
Hi there!



We’re a Real Estate Wholesale company hiring a GoHighLevel expert to manage our GoHighLevel platform in 2 areas: 



1. Building webpages and funnels using the GoHighLevel Builder > Extensive experience

2. Building and managing automations that handle all communications with Sellers and Buyers/Investors.



This is a hands-on role. You will build, run, and optimize funnels and multi-channel follow-up systems that nurture leads and increase appointments and contracts.



We need someone who’s FAST, RELIABLE, PERFORMANCE-DRIVEN, & HONEST with their time.



What you’ll do

You will build and manage landing pages, websites, and funnels using GoHighLevel Builder.

You will create and optimize forms, calendars, surveys, and lead capture flows inside GoHighLevel.

You will manage all communications for Sellers and Buyers/Investors through automated sequences.

You will build GoHighLevel Workflows including triggers, branching, stop rules, tagging, and routing.

You will build AI-assisted workflows using GPT-style tools to personalize messages, summarize conversations, detect intent, and route next steps.

You will manage multi-channel sequences including
, SMS, RVM (ringless voi
), and AI Voice follow-up.

You will own
 deliverability and sending health including domain warm-up, inbox placement, list hygiene, throttling, and spam prevention.

You will track performance weekly and improve results through testing and iteration.

You will maintain CRM hygiene including pipeline stages, tags, custom fields, tasks, and accurate logging of all touchpoints.



Requirements (must-have)

3+ years experience in GoHighLevel funnels, marketing automation, lifecycle communications, or CR
ssaging with hands-on execution.

Strong GoHighLevel experience including funnel/page building and workflow automation.

Strong AI + automation experience including prompt workflows, GPT personalization, and automation logic.

Experience with integrations including Zapier/Make, webhooks, and connecting external tools to GoHighLevel.

High-level English speaking and writing and strong copywriting for conversion.

Performance-driven mindset with comfort owning KPIs and improving them through testing.

Real estate experience is a big advantage and CRM experience is required.





Nice to have

Twilio and SMS best practices experience.

Experience with RVM providers and AI voice tools including routing and alerts.

Experience with GA4, GTM, Meta/Google pixels and basic attribution.



How to apply

Start your message with “I’m A GoHighLevel Wizard”.

Send a link to your CV.

Share links or screenshots of GoHighLevel funnels and workflows you built (please redact sensitive info).

Share 2 examples of AI or automation workflows you built and the results you achieved.

Send your monthly salary expectation in USD plus your availability and time zone.

Include a short summary of your GoHighLevel experience (funnels/pages, workflows, integrations).

Include a short summary of your CRM experience and what you built.

Add me via Teams, my ID is selashimon
